title: Four Wheel Steering Mechanism Project
description: Speed-adaptive 4WS system on a 1:5 scale vehicle model: rear servo actuator steers rear wheels counter-phase at low speed (turning radius −30%) and in-phase at high speed (stability +20%), Arduino Mega controller.
objective: Counter-phase rear steer at v < 3 km/h (model scale); in-phase at v > 6 km/h; potentiometer front-steer angle feedback; hall-effect speed sensing.
constraints: 12 V; Arduino Mega; 1:5 RC vehicle scale; BOM < ₹3000.
design_element_titles: DC Gear Motor with PWM Speed Control

element_usage_notes.Four-Wheel Steering — Rear Servo Actuato: Rear servo actuator; δ_r = k(v)·δ_f; k = −0.3 counter-phase, +0.1 in-phase.
element_usage_notes.DC Gear Motor with PWM Speed Control: Drive motors provide speed signal via wheel encoder to determine steering mode.
